name: Wizard Spider (Conti/Ryuk)
description: VALIDATED emulation plan for Wizard Spider, a Russia-based financially motivated threat group known for TrickBot (since 2016) and ransomware operations (Ryuk, Conti). Notable for targeting hospitals and major corporations.
validation_notes: VALIDATED against MITRE ATT&CK v19.1 - All techniques documented with evidence citations. Featured in MITRE Engenuity ATT&CK Evaluations 2022. v19 update: Defense Evasion split into Stealth (TA0005) and Defense Impairment (TA0112); existing tests re-tagged via crosswalk, v19-new attributions appended where Atomic Red Team coverage exists.
techniques_without_atomic_coverage: T1585.002 - Establish Accounts: Email Accounts (ProtonMail for ransom notes)
T1021 - Remote Services (WebDAV for Ryuk payloads)
T1588.002 - Obtain Capabilities: Tool (Empire, Cobalt Strike, Rubeus, AdFind, BloodHound)
T1588.003 - Obtain Capabilities: Code Signing Certificates (DigiCert, GlobalSign, COMOOD)

AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used net group "Domain admins" /DOMAIN and Get-ADComputer
Wizard Spider used Invoke-Inveigh PowerShell cmdlets for name service poisoning
Wizard Spider used HTTP for network communications

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider archived data into ZIP files on compromised machines
Wizard Spider used batch scripts with WMIC to execute BITSAdmin transfers

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider established persistence via HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Winlogon Helper DLL
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider established persistence using HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon
Wizard Spider used macros to execute PowerShell for malware download and lateral movement

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used cmd.exe to execute commands on victim's machine
Wizard Spider created local administrator accounts for persistence
Wizard Spider created new accounts within Active Directory

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider installed TrickBot as service named ControlServiceA

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Credentials from Password Stores: Windows Credential Manager
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used Invoke-WCMDump to enumerate Windows Credential Manager
Wizard Spider collected data from compromised hosts prior to exfiltration
Wizard Spider staged ZIP files in C:\PerfLogs\1\ and C:\User\1\

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Unencrypted Non-C2
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider exfiltrated victim information using FTP
Wizard Spider exfiltrated domain credentials and network enumeration over C2

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Cloud Storage
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider exfiltrated stolen data to various cloud storage providers
Wizard Spider exploited Zerologon (CVE-2020-1472) and EternalBlue (MS17-010)
Wizard Spider accessed victim networks using stolen VPN credentials

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: File and Directory Permissions Modification: Windows
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used icacls to modify access control on backup servers

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider shut down or uninstalled security applications
Wizard Spider used file deletion to remove modules and configurations
Wizard Spider transferred malicious payloads such as ransomware
Wizard Spider used WMIC and vssadmin to delete volume shadow copies
Wizard Spider used stolen credentials to copy tools into %TEMP% of domain controllers
Wizard Spider used scheduled tasks with legitimate names like WinDotNet, GoogleTask, Sysnetsf
Wizard Spider modified WDigest registry key to cache plaintext credentials in memory
Wizard Spider used 'net view' to locate mapped network shares

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Obfuscated Files or Information: Command Obfuscation
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used Base64 encoding to obfuscate Empire service and PowerShell commands
Wizard Spider dumped lsass.exe memory with LaZagne tool

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: OS Credential Dumping: Security Account Manager
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider acquired credentials from SAM/SECURITY registry hives
Wizard Spider exported ntds.dit Active Directory database using ntdsutil
Wizard Spider used spearphishing with macros to deliver Emotet, TrickBot, Bazar
Wizard Spider sent phishing emails with links to actor-controlled Google Drive documents
Wizard Spider used process injection to escalate privileges
Wizard Spider injected malicious DLLs into memory with read, write, execute permissions
Wizard Spider used RDP for lateral movement and interactive ransomware deployment
Wizard Spider used SMB to drop Cobalt Strike Beacon on domain controllers
Wizard Spider used WinRM to move laterally through victim network
Wizard Spider used AdFind, nltest/dclist, and Get-DataInfo.ps1 to enumerate domain computers
Wizard Spider used scheduled tasks to establish persistence for TrickBot
Wizard Spider used taskkill.exe and net.exe to stop backup, catalog, cloud services
Wizard Spider used WMI to identify anti-virus products on victim machine
Wizard Spider used Get-DataInfo.ps1 to collect installed backup software information

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used Rubeus, MimiKatz Kerberos module, and Invoke-Kerberoast to steal AES hashes
Wizard Spider used Digicert code-signing certificates for malware
Wizard Spider used rundll32.exe to deploy ransomware commands with WebDAV
Wizard Spider used Systeminfo and Get-ADComputer to collect host information
Wizard Spider used ipconfig and Get-ADComputer to collect IP address data
Wizard Spider used 'whoami' to identify local user and privileges
Wizard Spider used services.exe and PsExec batch scripts for lateral movement
Wizard Spider used Get-GPPPassword and Find-GPOPassword to find unsecured credentials

AtomicTests.TechniqueName: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Hash
AtomicTests.MitreReference: Wizard Spider used Invoke-SMBExec for pass-the-hash lateral movement
Wizard Spider lured victims into clicking malicious links via spearphishing
Wizard Spider lured victims to execute malware via spearphishing attachments with macros
Wizard Spider used valid credentials to access domain controllers
Wizard Spider used Domain Admin accounts for lateral movement
Wizard Spider used WMI and LDAP queries for discovery and lateral movement with WMIC batch scripts
TrickBot/Ryuk operators disabled Defender via PowerShell prior to Ryuk deployment

v19_changes.tactic_split: Defense Evasion split into Stealth (TA0005) + Defense Impairment (TA0112)
